mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 8.0 percent signals tightening labor market conditions that boost consumer spending power and corporate revenue growth, driving positive equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the monthly IC drops below zero for three consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with increasing residential mortgage debt indicate synchronized consumer credit expansion and durable goods demand, which jointly amplify corporate earnings expectations and lift S&P 500 prices over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative coefficient in a multivariate regression, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when both proxies rise simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in the industrial production index reflects recovering manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost inflation and supports equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns remain negative or flat during consecutive months of rising industrial production, or the rolling 3-year Spearman correlation falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP directly expands the aggregate earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, producing a persistent positive drift in equity returns over the subsequent trading week.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if quarterly GDP growth rates show no statistical relationship with 5-day forward returns (p > 0.10) or if the predictive R-squared remains below 0.01 over a 10-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly consumer price index increase exceeding 0.25 percent triggers market expectations of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral despite consecutive months of CPI acceleration above the threshold, or the forward return distribution shows no significant left skew during high-inflation months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm a self-reinforcing labor market expansion that drives wage growth and consumer demand, jointly lifting equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of observations, or the interaction coefficient becomes statistically insignificant when controlling for GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in housing starts exceeding 700 thousand units signals strong builder confidence and mortgage demand, which historically precedes positive S&P 500 returns over the following week as construction and related sectors drive index gains.
failure_condition: The signal yields negative 5-day forward returns in over 60% of triggered instances, or the rolling 1-year predictive accuracy drops below 52% during housing booms.
